releases.title: collectZ v3.8.20
releases.summary: This release adds a public homelab environment reference so `env.example` can stay minimal while operators still have a safe place to understand optional deployment settings.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `docs/wiki/48-Public-Homelab-Environment-Reference.md` for operator-safe public homelab settings.
Updated README and deployment docs to point public users at the new homelab-specific environment reference.
Marked the broad environment-variable page as a maintainer/runtime reference rather than the first public setup path.
Removed stale Portainer/deploy guidance that implied version metadata variables changed the public prebuilt image tag.
Removed public deploy guidance that recommended putting audit verbosity in the default Portainer environment.
Added the new public reference to public export surface validation.
Kept the Playwright browser-regression bypass out of public compose/docs while restoring the CI-only compose override that passes the per-run bypass token to the backend test stack.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.19
releases.summary: This release simplifies the public homelab Docker Compose file so it defaults directly to the GHCR `latest` images and only exposes the runtime environment values needed for normal homelab startup.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Changed public backend image to `ghcr.io/hkrewson/collectz-backend:latest`.
Changed public frontend image to `ghcr.io/hkrewson/collectz-frontend:latest`.
Removed public `IMAGE_REGISTRY`, `IMAGE_NAMESPACE`, and `IMAGE_TAG` indirection from the generated compose surface.
Removed provider/bootstrap tuning variables from the default public compose environment block.
Kept required database, session, encryption, CORS, reverse-proxy, secure-cookie, upload, and port settings in the public compose.
Tightened public export validation so image-source indirection variables do not return to public deploy docs, compose, or env examples.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.18
releases.summary: This release tightens the public homelab deployment surface by keeping env examples focused on required runtime values and removing testing/private controls from public compose/docs.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed the Playwright browser-regression bypass variable from the generated public compose file.
Tightened public export validation so public compose/env/docs cannot expose Playwright bypass controls, session bearer fallback controls, or private edition controls.
Clarified `env.example` around required secrets versus optional database and reverse-proxy settings.
Updated README wording so public homelab setup does not point users at private/platform edition controls.
Kept public compose defaulting to `NODE_ENV=production` and secure-cookie posture unless explicitly overridden for local HTTP testing.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.17
releases.summary: This release makes Capture Inbox rows explain why they need attention, using backend-derived review reasons.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `review_reasons` readback to capture items.
Derives reasons for replay conflicts, lookup problems, multiple choices, no matches, ready-to-add captures, and missing details.
Shows those reasons directly on Capture Inbox rows as compact text.
Documents the new readback in OpenAPI.
Preserves existing review tabs, source filters, batch summary, scanner shortcuts, and import/link actions.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.16
releases.summary: This release makes scanner-app captures easier to find and continue from inside Capture Inbox.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added backend source filtering for Capture Inbox so scanner-origin captures can be requested directly.
Added source counts for scanner and web captures in Capture Inbox responses.
Added a compact scanner shortcut on Capture Inbox when scanner captures are waiting.
Added a source selector with Scanner app and Web capture filters.
Marks scanner-origin rows as Scanner app in capture row details.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.15
releases.summary: This release makes batch scanning easier to wrap up by keeping a compact summary on Capture Inbox after a batch is stopped.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a completed batch summary to Capture Inbox.
Shows added, linked, needs-choice, no-match, and failed counts after batch mode stops.
Keeps a short recent outcome list so the user can see what just happened.
Adds direct review actions for choices and no-match captures.
Keeps the existing batch scan flow and exact ISBN auto-add behavior intact.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.14
releases.summary: This release makes batch scanning faster when Capture Inbox finds one safe exact ISBN book match. Multiple lookup candidates still stay in the editor for user choice.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added safe exact ISBN match detection to Capture Inbox.
Batch scan now automatically saves and imports a single exact ISBN book match.
Normal one-off captures still show the candidate list in place.
Single exact ISBN rows now use clearer `Add exact ISBN` / `Link exact ISBN` copy.
Kept multi-candidate ISBN/UPC lookups as explicit user choice.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.13
releases.summary: This release makes Capture Inbox easier to work through by adding review filters for captures that need a decision, are ready to add, have no match, are missing details, or have provider/replay problems.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added server-backed `review_filter` support to `GET /api/capture-items`.
Added capture review counts for all review filters.
Added Capture Inbox review tabs: `All`, `Needs choice`, `Ready to add`, `No match`, `Missing details`, and `Problems`.
Kept all capture records on the existing Capture Inbox page instead of creating a separate queue.
Updated OpenAPI to document the new query parameter and response counts.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.12
releases.summary: This release makes Capture Inbox faster for scanning several books or items in a row by adding an explicit batch scan mode.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a `Batch scan` action to Capture Inbox.
Batch mode keeps the editor open and launches the next camera barcode scan after saving.
The editor now shows a compact batch-session readback with saved, added, and review counts.
`Save and scan next` keeps unresolved captures in Capture Inbox for later review.
`Add to library` and `Link` continue to the next scan while batch mode is active.
Normal one-off `New capture` behavior remains available.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.11
releases.summary: This release shortens the Capture Inbox barcode workflow by showing lookup candidates immediately in the new-capture editor after a barcode or ISBN is scanned or entered.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Capture Inbox can run the scanner barcode lookup contract before the capture row is saved.
New-capture lookup results appear inline with candidate title, source, type, and library state.
A selected result can be added or linked through the existing Capture Inbox import path.
Captures saved for review now keep lookup matches and lookup status on the capture row.
Added a `Save and scan next` action so scanning another item does not require returning to the inbox list.
